findings.riskNarrative: Web browser data loading may be possible, due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server.
findings.remediation:
Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner (using IP address white-listing, for instance).
Configure the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" HTTP header to a more restrictive s

findings.riskNarrative: The response does not protect against 'ClickJacking' attacks. It should include either Content-Security-Policy with 'frame-ancestors' directive or X-Frame-Options.
findings.remediation:
Modern Web browsers support the Content-Security-Policy and X-Frame-Options HTTP headers. Ensure one of them is set on all web pages returned by your site/app.
If you expect the page to be framed only

findings.alertName: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
findings.riskNarrative: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. These attacks a
findings.remediation: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

findings.remediation:
Ensure that the application/web server sets the Content-Type header appropriately, and that it sets the X-Content-Type-Options header to 'nosniff' for all web pages.

findings.remediation: Validate that the response does not contain sensitive, personal or user-specific information. If it does, consider the use of the following HTTP response headers, to limit, or prevent the content bein

findings.riskNarrative: The cache-control header has not been set properly or is missing, allowing the browser and proxies to cache content. For static assets like css, js, or image files this might be intended, however, the
findings.remediation: For secure content, ensure the cache-control HTTP header is set with "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate". If an asset should be cached consider setting the directives "public, max-age, immutable".

findings.plainEnglish: The low color contrast between the text and background in the heading makes it difficult for users with low vision or in bright lighting conditions to read the content. This can also impact users with color vision deficiencies.
findings.affectedUsers: Users with low vision, color vision deficiencies, and users in bright lighting conditions
findings.remediation: Increase the color contrast between the text and background to meet the WCAG 2.0 AA minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1. This can be achieved by using a darker text color or a lighter background color.
